title: Write Unit Tests
description: Generate focused unit tests for a function, including edge cases.
content:
Write unit tests for this code using {{framework}}:

```
{{code}}
```

Cover: the happy path, boundary values, empty/null inputs, and error conditions. Each test should assert one behavior and have a name that reads as a sentence. Avoid testing implementation details — test observable behavior. List any case you deliberately left out and why.

title: Edge Case Enumeration
description: Brainstorm the edge cases a piece of functionality must handle.
content:
For this functionality, enumerate the edge cases worth testing: {{functionality}}

Think across categories: empty/zero/one/many, boundaries and off-by-one, null/undefined/missing, very large inputs, duplicates, ordering, concurrency, time zones/locales, encoding, and malformed input. For each, state the expected behavior. Flag any where the correct behavior is genuinely unclear and needs a product decision.

title: Test-Driven Development
description: Drive an implementation from tests, red-green-refactor.
content:
Let's build {{feature}} using strict TDD.

Step 1: Write the smallest failing test for the next slice of behavior — stop and show me only the test.
Step 2: After I confirm, write the minimum code to make it pass.
Step 3: Refactor if needed, keeping tests green.

Proceed one cycle at a time. Don't write implementation ahead of a test that demands it.

title: Property-Based Tests
description: Design property-based tests that assert invariants over generated inputs.
content:
Design property-based tests for: {{code_or_function}}

Identify the invariants that must hold for ALL valid inputs (e.g. round-trip encode/decode equals identity, output is always sorted, length is preserved). For each property, specify the input generator and any shrinking concerns. Use {{framework}}. Note which properties caught real bugs in your experience with similar code.

title: Increase Test Coverage
description: Find untested branches and add the tests that matter.
content:
Here's code with weak test coverage:

```
{{code}}
```

Identify the branches, conditions, and error paths that aren't exercised. Prioritize them by risk, not by coverage percentage. Write tests for the high-risk gaps first. Explicitly call out any code that doesn't need a test (trivial, generated, or better covered by an integration test).

title: Write an Integration Test
description: Test how components work together against real-ish dependencies.
content:
Write an integration test for this flow: {{flow}}

Decide what to use real vs. faked: prefer real for the components under test, fakes/containers for slow or external systems. Cover the success path and at least one realistic failure (timeout, partial write, bad response). Ensure the test sets up and tears down its own state so it's repeatable and isolated.

title: Diagnose a Flaky Test
description: Find and fix the source of test flakiness.
content:
This test passes sometimes and fails others:

```
{{test}}
```

Identify the source of non-determinism: shared state, timing/sleeps, ordering dependence, real clock, randomness, network, or resource leaks. Propose a fix that makes it deterministic without weakening what it verifies. If the right fix is to make the *code* more testable, say so.

title: Review Test Quality
description: Critique a test suite for what it actually guarantees.
content:
Review these tests for quality, not quantity:

```
{{tests}}
```

For each test ask: does it test behavior or implementation? Would it fail if the code broke? Would it pass for the wrong reasons? Flag tautological assertions, over-mocking, missing edge cases, and tests that just restate the code. Recommend which to keep, fix, or delete.
